Title: Apocarotenoid signaling regulates meristem activity and shapes shoot and root lateral organ formation in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACS1 accumulation in clb5 reduces CLV3 expression in the SAM, consistent with compromised stem cell maintenance; low light restores CLV3 expression and SAM activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CLV3:GUS is reduced progressively in clb5 under standard light. 2. Transfer to low light restores CLV3 expression and can induce ectopic CLV3 domains. 3. Recovery of CLV3 correlates with renewed leaf production and laminar leaf development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Shoot apical meristem stem cell domain
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard light versus low light; NFZ-treated wild type used as control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as developmental readout rather than manipulated causal gene

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ACS1 accumulation suppresses PIN1 expression in developing leaves, disrupting auxin distribution and contributing to radial leaf morphology; low light restores PIN1:GFP in emerging leaves
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN transporter genes are downregulated in clb5 transcriptomes, especially at stage 1.02. 2. proPIN1:PIN1:GFP becomes nearly undetectable in 10-d-old clb5 leaves under standard light. 3. Low light restores PIN1 expression and laminar morphology in a subset of emerging leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf vasculature and developing leaves; previously also altered in roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard light versus low light; NFZ-treated wild type comparison
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Readout of ACS1 effects on auxin transport, not direct ACS1 biosynthetic gene

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WOX5 expression persists in clb5/spc1-2 QC cells, but ACS1 accumulation disrupts QC spatial organization and causes ectopic/misaligned WOX5-positive cells
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WOX5 marker is largely normal at 5 d but altered spatially at 10 d in clb5/spc1-2. 2. QC identity is retained despite disorganization. 3. ACS1 affects RAM niche organization more than WOX5 expression presence/absence.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SCR expression remains detectable in clb5/spc1-2 roots, indicating preserved cell identity despite ACS1-induced QC and columella disorganization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. proSCR:SCR:YFP pattern is similar to controls at 5 d. 2. In 10-d-old ACS1-accumulating mutants, SCR remains present while niche organization is abnormal. 3. ACS1 likely perturbs spatial organization rather than abolishing QC/endodermal identity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: QC and endodermal cell lineage in RAM

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: More desaturated cis-carotene intermediates en route to lycopene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Conditionally active plastid-derived signal regulating plastid biogenesis, meristem maintenance, leaf expansion, root apical meristem organization, lateral root initiation, columella starch accumulation, and gravitropism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with/supplies precursor pool for ACS1 formation by causing precursor accumulation when defective
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ZDS activity in clb5/spc1-2 causes phytofluene and ζ-carotene accumulation, promotes ACS1 overaccumulation under standard light, and leads to radial leaves, SAM/RAM defects, reduced lateral root initiation, impaired columella starch accumulation, and delayed gravitropism; low light reduces ACS1 synthesis and reverses many defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. clb5 and spc1-2 are ZDS-defective mutants that accumulate ACS1 precursors and overproduce ACS1 under standard light. 2. Their developmental defects in shoot and root are dynamically reversible under low light, linking phenotype to ACS1 production rather than irreversible damage. 3. ZDS shows tissue-specific expression in SAM and RAM, supporting localized ACS1 precursor production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces additional desaturation into ζ-carotene intermediates in the linear carotenoid pathway toward lycopene formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in SAM and RAM; in RAM enriched in columella cells and lateral root cap, low in central cylinder/endodermis/cortex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In 10-d-old root apical meristem, ZDS:YFP is abundant in columella cells and lateral root cap
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Standard light versus low light; norflurazon blocks upstream pathway; singlet oxygen contributes to nonenzymatic cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames ZDS mainly through mutant alleles clb5 and spc1-2 as the causal biosynthetic locus underlying ACS1 overaccumulation; ACS1 itself remains chemically unresolved in this study
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ZDS loss causes severe albino and developmental defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating ZDS or its spatial expression could modulate cis-carotene-derived signaling, but severe pleiotropy makes direct disruption unsuitable for breeding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (clb5, spc1-2); low-light phenotypic rescue; carotenoid profiling cited; RNA-seq; reporter localization; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE152252; NCBI BioProject PRJNA638809
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot apical meristem; root apical meristem; developing leaves; columella region

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plastid-derived developmental signal when produced from cis-carotene precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PDS3 is required upstream of ACS1 precursor formation; pds3 blocks carotenoid biosynthesis upstream of ZDS, preventing ACS1 production and serving as a non-ACS1 albino control lacking fingerlike leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDS3 acts upstream of ZDS and ACS1 precursor formation. 2. pds3 mutants do not show ACS1 overaccumulation-associated radial leaves, supporting the causal role of ACS1 rather than carotenoid deficiency alone. 3. PDS3-GFP localizes to plastids and shows RAM tissue-specific accumulation distinct from ZDS.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates phytoene to phytofluene and subsequent cis-carotene intermediates upstream of ζ-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in SAM central zone and higher in rib zone; in RAM present in central cylinder, endodermis, and cortex, lower in columella
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PDS3:GFP detected in plastids in RAM, enriched outside columella relative to ZDS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Norflurazon inhibits PDS3; standard light versus low light
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as an upstream pathway block control to discriminate ACS1-specific effects from general carotenoid deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PDS3 causes albino phenotype and broad carotenoid deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct disruption is unsuitable due to severe carotenoid deficiency, but useful experimentally to suppress ACS1 formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison (pds3 vs clb5/spc1-2); promoter-protein fusion localization; NFZ treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE152252; NCBI BioProject PRJNA638809

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirect precursor supply to carotenoid-derived signaling molecules including ACS1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DXS1 shows broad RAM plastid accumulation, supporting provision of isoprenoid precursors for localized ACS1 precursor synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXS1 is expressed broadly in RAM plastids including QC cells. 2. Its expression pattern supports precursor supply throughout the meristem. 3. The study uses DXS1 mainly to map spatial competence for ACS1 precursor biosynthesis rather than as a mutant causal gene.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate in the MEP pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low but detectable across SAM/RAM; broadly present in RAM including QC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: DXS1:YFP accumulates in plastids across most RAM tissues including QC cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included to support tissue-specific ACS1 precursor synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream handle for modulating carotenoid-derived signals, but this paper provides no direct engineering evidence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native promoter translational fusion imaging; single-cell transcriptomics mining
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Single-cell RNA-seq from published datasets cited; RNA-seq: GEO GSE152252; BioProject PRJNA638809

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Developmental signal affecting plastid biogenesis and meristem function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CCD4 contributes to ACS1 synthesis from accumulated ZDS substrates; loss of CCD4 previously caused only temporary phenotypic reversion, whereas low light gives stronger rescue by reducing ROS-mediated cleavage as well
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD4 participates in ACS1 formation from ZDS substrates. 2. ACS1 synthesis is partially redundant, involving CCD4 and nonenzymatic ROS-mediated cleavage. 3. Reduced ACS1 under low light explains stronger rescue than CCD4 loss alone.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves accumulated cis-carotene precursors to generate ACS1 or its immediate apocarotenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light intensity and ROS/singlet oxygen levels modulate enzymatic versus nonenzymatic cleavage contribution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in this paper is based partly on previous studies cited; still central to the ACS1 biosynthetic model used to interpret results
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating CCD4 may incompletely suppress ACS1 because nonenzymatic cleavage remains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be targeted together with light/ROS management to modulate cis-carotene-derived signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic inference from prior ccd4 loss studies combined with current low-light rescue comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Specific tissues and developmental stages including meristems
